title: Hello RedwoodSDK
author: Tom Preston-Werner
body:
# Hello RedwoodSDK

This is our very first blog post. Welcome to the **RedwoodSDK** blog!

RedwoodSDK lets you build fast, server-rendered applications on Cloudflare Workers.

## Features

- Server-side rendering
- React Server Components
- Edge-first deployment

```js
console.log('Hello from the edge!');
```

Stay tuned for more posts.

title: Cloudflare Edge Tips
author: Sarah Chen
body:
# Cloudflare Edge Tips

Running at the edge means thinking differently about latency, data locality, and cold starts.

## Tip 1: Minimize Cold Starts

Keep your Worker bundle small and use minimal dependencies.

## Tip 2: Cache Aggressively

Use the Cache API to store responses at the edge:

```js
const cache = caches.default;
await cache.put(request, response);
```

## Tip 3: Use KV for Config

Workers KV is great for read-heavy configuration data.

That's it for now — happy edge computing!

title: React RSC 101
author: Lee Robinson
body:
# React RSC 101

React Server Components (RSC) let you render components on the server, sending only the result to the client.

## Why RSC?

- **Less JavaScript** shipped to the browser
- **Direct data access** from server-side databases
- **Streaming** support out of the box

## A Simple Server Component

```jsx
export async function Post({ id }) {
  const post = await db.post.findUnique({ where: { id } });
  return <article>{post.content}</article>;
}
```

## Client Boundaries

Use `"use client"` to mark components that need interactivity.

RSC is the future of React — embrace it!
